title.en: Patrick Ponce 7.05 US Nats '19 Winning Average Analysis

content.en:
Patrick Ponce 7.05 US Nats '19 Winning Average Analysis

Jayden McNeill

It's been a while since I've done one of these, and I thought Patrick would make for a fantastic case study for a few reasons

 

For one thing, even though he's quite young (well, maybe not so much anymore), and although he turns quite fast, he's actually an EXTREMELY intelligent solver

 

Even in this high pressure situation, his maturity as a cuber really shows

 

I also wanted to use him as a small shameless plug for a group we made a while ago

 

  

His fundamentals (fingertricks, inspection game, alg/solution choices etc) are all being done at a very high level, and you really shouldn't be at all surprised if he's the next person to sub 6 avg officially

  

 

 

R2 B' U2 B F U2 F' L F' R' D2 B' R' D F' U2 B F2

 

 

x // inspection


F R2 U' L2' // cross - It's a 4 move cross, so there isn't really much to say about the cross itself, but...

 

The fact that Patrick was able to ignore the orange yellow block, something that a lot of cubers notoriously get hung up on, shows his maturity 

 

Doing something like U L' U2 into some meh Xcross finish isn't terrible obviously, but since he saw a keyhole case as his first pair (aka the next best thing from getting a free pair, unless it's the corner with the cross colour sticker facing up), it really didn't make much sense to go for something else

 

Like I've said and been saying, Patrick's experience as a 3x3 solver really shows with little things like this

 


(U' D) R' U R u' // 1st pair - Forcing the pair to go into BL instead of BR (or maybe just making pair 2 rotationless... or both!) was a great move on Patrick's part here

 

Doing U D R' U' R u' would have meant staying in neutral grip, which is literally the only thing he did "wrong" in my opinion

 

Very minor detail obviously, but just wanted to show that even great solutions have room for improvement ;)

 


R U' R2' U' R // 2nd pair - A lot of cubers would do U R' U' R U' R' U' R here, but Patrick being the G that he is recognized the free slot would allow for a 5 move solution instead of a 7-8 mover

 


U2 R U' R' U' R U' R2' F R U' F' // 3rd pair - Pretty standard solution for the most part, but the ending is something that I honestly haven't even thought about doing 

 

When you have an FR slot case that's going to end in U R U' R', but the FL slot has a flipped edge, doing R' F R U' F' makes a lot of sense since it's nearly as fast as U R U' R', but it gives you a rotationless last pair!

 

I probably would have just done the pair normally and then y' into ZBLS>ZBLL, and I think for me that might be faster, but that's obviously an exception to the rule

 

Even then, I think I might just have to start doing R' F R U' F' more

 


U' U' L' U L U L' U' L // 4th pair - I like that he didn't do U L' U' L U' L' U' L, which is a habit you'll see from a lot of cubers who do F2L *too* intuitively

 

Simple cases cannot be overlooked! 3 U' turns in a row is U G L Y

 


U' U2 R U2' (R' l') U l F' R U2' R' // OLL - Standard stuff

 

OLLCP is possible here but overrated IMO since it requires a rotation (and also R U2' R2' F R F' R U2' R' is ridiculously fast, like sub .5 with perfect fingertricks)

 


U' R' U r' F2 R F' R' F2 R r U' // PLL - Recently I've been liking r' F R' F2 r U' r' F2 r R a lot more than the alg Patrick used, due to the fact that you can start and end in home grip a lot easier 

 

That said, if it weren't for that alg existing, I'd use what Patrick uses (and still do for a select few AUF's, but not this one)

 

 

 

R' F' L' F' B2 U L B' R2 D B2 L U2 L' D2 R U2 B2 R2

 

 

x' y' // inspection


R' F L F // cross - Again, keeping it simple and not over complicating things, which is honestly a real skill

 


U R U' R' U' R U R' // 1st pair - U' R' U R2 U R' is less moves, sure, but you have to either leave home grip or do awkward U turns, which isn't necessarily better (especially in a big final)

 


y' U R U R' // 2nd pair - Free pair, duh

 


y' U2 U' U R U2' R' R' F R F' // 3rd pair - I kinda get the sledge if it was to misorient the last F2L edge and avoid a rotation, but I'm like 99% sure Patrick thought he was doing edge control when he wasn't

 

No biggie since cancelling into the sledge might have made the pair faster overall, but ideally he would have done:

 

* y' U2 R U2' R' U2 R U' R' U' R' F R U' F' *

 

Moving on...

 


U' r' F' r U2' r' F r // 4th pair - If this were a righty pair I might have done the free pair with a U2 insert instead to give a slightly better OLL, but I don't think lefty P is better than lefty Fatsune (and seeing OLS info from the left isn't worth it a lot of the time anyways imo)

 


U R' F' r U' r' F2 R // OLL - U R U2' R' U2 R' F R F' avoids diag here without unnecessarily slowing down the OLL in terms of exec, but recognizing Sune/Antisune CP isn't always instant for some angles, plus diag PLL's really aren't that bad (or at least don't have to be)

 


U' R' U R' d' R' F' R U' U R U' R' U R' F R F U // PLL - I figure that by now Patrick's on the R' U R' U' R D' R' D R' U D' R2 U' R2' D R2 train by now (if not... brah)

 

Keep in mind, most top cubers learned the new swag Vperm AT Worlds, so using it in a big final after so little time is a pretty ridiculous risk to take just to save milliseconds 

 

 

D' F2 U2 L2 D B2 U' F' L U2 B' D R' B F2 L' U' F'

 

 

y2 z // inspection


(D' U) R' F D F' // cross - Simple again

 

I think some cubers might fixate over the fact that the yellow edge is already solved, and ignore the fact that blue is one move away from solved AND makes the overall cross a lot better

 


R' U' R y L U L' // 1st pair - Questionable that he didn't just do y U' L' U L2 U L'

 

Maybe he has a reason, but for now I think y into that solution was better for sure (would have also made pair 2 a better case simply by removing that flipped edge)

 


U L' U' L U' F U F' // 2nd pair - F U F' is a tricky insert to fingertrick, but if you can do it this is one of these cases where it helps a lot IMO

 

Of course, had he rotated instead and saw the green white pair, he could have done a simple multislot which I actually talked about in a recent video

 

The trouble is, either he would have had to make BL his last pair (ew), or do ugly fingertricks, so in the overall context of the solve it's not as great as you might think

 


R U R2' U2' R y U' L' U L // 3rd pair - Obviously since this is the worst solve of the average, there's going to be more things to nitpick, but I really don't understand why he wouldn't do U R U2' R' U F' U' F or something similar here (even y R U2' R' U2 L' U' L or y R U2' R2' F R U F' are better I think)

 

Pretty sure this was just jitters

 


U U' R U R' U R R' U R R' U' R U' R' // 4th pair - This was definitely jitters lmao

 

Also VLS flex time - F' r U2' R' U r' F2 R !!!

 


U F R U R' U' F' // EO - I feel like I might have given Patrick this idea, and I don't like it here lmfao

 

Even though this OLL is notoriously lockup city for a lot of cubers, it's low movecount and here it skips CP

 


U' U F R U' R' U' R U2 R' U' R U' R' U' R U2 R' U' F' U // ZBLL - Yeah I don't think the risk paid off

 

At the very least, I can appreciate him going for it, and there are absolutely times where it can pay off (even with diagU ZBLL)

 

Here it was just a mix of getting unlucky and slightly poor decision making with regards to the OLLCP

 

 

F2 U F B2 U' R2 L' U B' U' F' L2 B2 L2 B2 L' D2 L' D2

 

 

y x2 // inspection


D F R D2 R u // cross - Nice touch with the u instead of a more traditional D, allowing the pair to be rotationless

 


R U2' R' U2 R U' R' // 1st pair - This is just a random ass multislot I know (similar to one from before), but...

 

* R U2' R' U' R U' R' y R' F R U' F' *

 


y' U' R U2' R' L U' // 2nd pair - Filling up BL instead of FR, and leaving last two slots in front

 

Good look ahead management

 


U U L2' U' L2 U' L' // 3rd pair - And a nice cancellation too, though he could have cancelled more had he recognized it sooner (gotta be slick with the fingertricks though, otherwise you should just spam L' U L U' L' U L U2' L' U L)

 


y' U' R' R R' U' R U R' U2' R // 4th pair - U r U' R' U R U r' plz

 

Or for ZBLS, r' F' r U r U' r' U F ;)

 

Or F' r' F' r U2 F for worse ZBLS

 

At the very least, I'm glad he inserted the pair with U2 instead of an ugly R' U R which requires a regrip (yet another thing I've talked about in the past)

 


R' F' r L' L U' L' U2 l // OLL - DeJaVu

 


U' x' R U' R' D R U R' D' R U R' D R U' R' D' // PLL - Some people are jumping on the R' U' R' D' R U' R' D R U R' D' R U R' D R2 train

 

I'm not one of them (not yet at least)

 

Neither is Patrick

 

 

R' D2 F2 U2 L2 F2 L' D2 U2 L B' D2 U' R F2 L' D2 U2

 

 

z // inspection


(U' D) r U' R U R // cross - I like that he didn't force himself to do the whole cross with the blue center on bottom the entire time, allowing for better fingertricks

 

Would've been better to see him end with R U' R though, which makes the first pair equally speedy whilst in home grip, but the transition from R U' R into that pair probably negates those benefits anyways

 


U' R' U' R R' R U' R' U R // 1st pair - Pretty standard stuff

 


y' R U' R' U' L' U' L // 2nd pair - Again, very standard

 


(U R U' R')3 // 3rd pair
U' R' U R U2' R' U R // 4th pair - This is one of those last two pair situations that's going to be done with PSF2L in the next 1-2 years by more top cubers

 

Better way to do last 2 pairs here:

 

* R' U2' R D U2' R' U2 R U' R' U R D' * (Can also insert last pair with U2 to give better OLL + a 2 move cancel, also unintentionally making things more home grip friendly)

 

You can also insert the corner other ways to get similarly efficient finishes

 


U R' F R U R' F' R F U' F' // OLL - Best way to do this OLL period

 


U x R' U R' D D R U' R' D2 R2 // PLL - National Champion ! ! !

algs: R2 B' U2 B F U2 F' L F' R' D2 B' R' D F' U2 B F2
R' F' L' F' B2 U L B' R2 D B2 L U2 L' D2 R U2 B2 R2
D' F2 U2 L2 D B2 U' F' L U2 B' D R' B F2 L' U' F'
F2 U F B2 U' R2 L' U B' U' F' L2 B2 L2 B2 L' D2 L' D2
R' D2 F2 U2 L2 F2 L' D2 U2 L B' D2 U' R F2 L' D2 U2
